items.question: My phone ___ stolen on the train. (informal)
explanations: Correct! Get passive = 'got' + participle: 'got stolen'.
'Was got' mixes be and get.
'Gets stolen' is present, not this past event.
'Getting stolen' can't stand alone here.

items.question: They ___ married last summer.
explanations: Correct! 'Get married' is a set phrase: 'got married'.
'Were got married' is wrong.
'Get married' is present; 'last summer' needs the past.
'Gotten married' isn't standard British usage here.

items.question: Did you ___ paid this month?
explanations: Correct! Questions use 'do' + base 'get': 'Did you get paid?'
After 'did', use the base form 'get', not 'got'.
'Did you were' is not correct.
'Did you been' is not correct.

items.question: He ___ hurt playing rugby.
explanations: Correct! A sudden event: 'got hurt'.
'Was got hurt' mixes be and get.
'Getting hurt' can't stand alone here.
'Gets hurt' is present, not this past event.

items.question: I didn't ___ invited to the party.
explanations: Correct! Negatives use 'didn't' + base 'get': 'didn't get invited'.
After 'didn't', use 'get', not 'got'.
'Didn't was' is not correct.
'Didn't been' is not correct.

items.question: Hurry up and ___ dressed!
explanations: Correct! 'Get dressed' is a set phrase (imperative 'get').
'Got dressed' is past, not an order.
'Be got dressed' is wrong.
'Getting dressed' isn't an imperative here.

items.question: Which sentence is more FORMAL?
options: The samples were tested in the lab.
The samples got tested in the lab.
The samples get tested in the lab.
The samples were got tested in the lab.
explanations: Correct! The be passive is the formal choice: 'were tested'.
'Got tested' is informal.
'Get tested' is informal and present.
'Were got tested' is grammatically wrong.

items.question: Careful, or you'll ___ lost in the old town.
explanations: Correct! 'Get lost' is a set phrase: 'you'll get lost'.
'Be got lost' is wrong.
After 'will', use the base 'get', not 'got'.
'Getting lost' doesn't follow 'will'.

items.question: Correct this: 'He was got promoted last year.'
options: He was got promoted last year.
He did got promoted last year.
explanations: Correct! Get passive: 'He got promoted' (or 'was promoted').
'Was got promoted' mixes be and get.
'Did got' is wrong — use 'got' or 'did get'.
'Gets' is present, not last year.

items.question: When is the get passive most natural?
options: In informal speech, for things that happen to us.
explanations: Correct! It's informal, for sudden/unwanted events and set phrases.
Formal writing prefers the be passive.
Legal writing uses the be passive.
It's used in statements, questions and negatives alike.
